surface.name: Your First Render Deploy, Dashboard Web Service
surface.selection_basis: Render's Your First Render Deploy tutorial is the official first-app path. It documents free resources with no payment required, and the Dashboard tab is the first deployment method shown for creating a Web Service from a connected Git repo and opening the Live onrender.com URL.

alternatives_considered.name: Static Site on the same tutorial
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Peer service type for fully static apps; this reconstruction uses the Web Service path for server-side apps.

alternatives_considered.name: Render CLI deploy tab
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Peer deployment method on the same page; requires installing the CLI before first deploy.

alternatives_considered.name: Language/framework quickstarts
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Example-app variants after the general first-deploy tutorial.

entry_point.developer_intent: Sign up for Render, connect a Git provider, deploy a server-side Web Service from a repository, and open the Live onrender.com URL.
assumed_prior_state: A GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket repository already contains the web app to deploy.
No Render account or Live Web Service is assumed.
No Render CLI install is assumed for the Dashboard path.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Your first deploy is Live and the app opens at its onrender.com URL
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer's Web Service deploy is Live and serves its root content from the assigned onrender.com URL.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The service shows Live status and opening the onrender.com URL returns the deployed app's root-path content.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Your First Render Deploy walks signup, Git connection, Web Service creation, Deploy, then opening the deployed app; free resources require no payment.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The tutorial's documented terminal state is a successful first deploy that the developer can open. Signup, Git authorization, or form submission alone are not first success. Service-creation form fields are one configuration step with required details, not separate path steps.

prerequisites.requirement: A GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket account with a repository containing the web app; this route uses GitHub.
Permission to authorize Render to access the selected repositories.
A server-side web app that listens for HTTP requests and binds its public HTTP server to 0.0.0.0 on the port Render provides.
The application build command, start command, and any required environment variables.
A payment method is not required for the tutorial Free instance path.

primary_path.action: Open Your First Render Deploy and follow the Dashboard deployment path for a Web Service.
details: The tutorial states it uses free Render resources and no payment is required.
primary_path.success_signal: Signup, Git connection, and Deploy sections are visible.

primary_path.action: Sign up for a free Render account.
primary_path.success_signal: The Dashboard and Account Settings are reachable.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Follow the displayed account recovery path.

primary_path.action: Connect a Git provider under Account Settings → Git Deployment Credentials.
details: Add credential and authorize GitHub (or GitLab/Bitbucket).
Grant access to the repositories needed for deploy.
primary_path.success_signal: The provider appears under Git Deployment Credentials.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Re-authorize if repository access is missing.

primary_path.action: Create a Web Service from the connected Git repository.
details: In the Dashboard, click New and select Web Service.
Select the repository and click Connect.
Complete the service form: Name, Region, Branch, Language/runtime, Build Command, Start Command, Free instance type, and any required environment variables.
primary_path.input: Connected Git provider and app repository
primary_path.output: Configured Web Service ready to deploy
primary_path.success_signal: The Deploy action is available on the completed form.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Correct runtime, build, and start commands if validation blocks submit.

primary_path.action: Click Deploy to create the Web Service and start the first build.
primary_path.success_signal: Deploy logs are visible for the new service.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Inspect build logs if the deploy fails.

primary_path.action: Wait until the deploy is Live, then open the service's onrender.com URL and confirm the app's root content.
details: Free web services can spin down after inactivity and spin back up on the next request.
primary_path.output: Verified Live Web Service at its public URL
primary_path.success_signal: The service is Live and the onrender.com URL serves the root-path content.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait for build/start completion; fix build or start command failures from logs.

branches.condition: The app is fully static HTML/CSS/JS.
branches.path: Create a Static Site instead of a Web Service and set Build Command plus Publish Directory.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Same Live URL terminal with static-site fields.

branches.condition: The developer prefers the CLI tab.
branches.path: Install the Render CLI, run render login, then render services create with the documented flags including --plan free.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds CLI installation before the same Live deploy boundary.

friction_gates.description: A Render account is required before Dashboard deploy actions.
The developer must authorize Render to access the selected Git repositories.
The tutorial requires choosing Web Service or Static Site, then completing service-specific form fields.
Build command, start command, runtime, and any required environment variables must match the application.
The first build and deploy must finish before the service is Live.
Free web services spin down after 15 minutes of inactivity and spin back up on the next request.

time_to_first_success.value: Run your web app in minutes (vendor claim; not independently measured)

uncertainties.question: Exact interactive signup fields on dashboard.render.com/register.
uncertainties.impact: Could add undocumented form fields before the first Dashboard session.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The tutorial says signup is fast and free without publishing the interactive field contract.

excluded_after_success: Custom domains and TLS beyond the default onrender.com URL
Background Workers, Cron Jobs, and other service types
